ogTitle: McEwens Beach vs McIntosh Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in McEwens Beach and McIntosh Creek.
heading: McEwens Beach vs McIntosh Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between McEwens Beach, Queensland and McIntosh Creek, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: McEwens Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to McIntosh Creek (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is McEwens Beach or McIntosh Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: McEwens Beach has a flood risk score of 50/100, while McIntosh Creek scores 20/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: McEwens Beach scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. McIntosh Creek scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in McEwens Beach or McIntosh Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. McEwens Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
